sentences.text: Hello?
Hello!
Hello, good morning, how are you?
Hi, I'm good, how are you?
Doing well.
How's the day going so far?
It's going!
We're preparing for, Marketing Summit, so, it's been a little bit chaotic with everything going on, and the acquisitions, and it's been a little crazy.
Just a small little acquisition there for you guys.
Yeah, exactly.
Exactly.
I mean, acquiring 1,500 properties is not easy?
I know.
I know.
Hopefully we make it look easy.
Hopefully we make it look like that.
That's so cool, and I feel like we've talked to… maybe it's David or Saad, but there hasn't really been any announcement about it, has there?
No, Ryan made an announcement company-wide probably 2 weeks ago, but they've kind of kept it quiet.

We have an introduction today with the FPI marketing team, so I think they're just kind of, like, letting everyone get their feet wet and, you know, get acclimated, and then maybe there'll… there'll be something, a greater announcement that's put out there, but… Yeah, everything's been kind of kept under wraps a little.
That's cool.
NASA, yourself.
I'm sorry?
How long have you been an asset yourself?
Oh my goodness, I think it's been about 2 years.
I came over with the first communities.
Oh, that's right.

Yeah, I was with them for, like, 13 years, so yeah, that was, it's been a really good adjustment, though, I've, I've actually… I love Asset, and, you know, obviously kept a lot of, the same folks that came over from the marketing team, with First Community, so… It's been… it's been really good.
Nice.
Well, we love that team.
We work closely with Monica, and we've worked with Jen as well, and they're all.
Okay, yeah, I worked with Jen for many a year, and then Monica actually took over my spot, after I had my baby.
I moved out of New Deb, because I just couldn't travel to the extent that's needed, for that position, but yeah, no, that's great to hear.
I didn't know that you worked closely with them, so fantastic.
Okay.
Yeah, we've been working with Asset for, what has it been now, Morgan?
3 years?
Yep.
At least 2 years, but specifically the Google product, it's been the last… a little over a year now.
Okay.
Yep.
Alright, well, very good.
Yeah, so I could guess we can just start.
So I know you and I chatted a while ago.
I was like.
7 months ago, so I don't expect you to remember some of it.

But I guess just how… how much you know about the product that we've been talking with Saad and David about, and if you wanted us to do, like, a run-through, or if we wanted to mostly talk, like, the integrations onboarding piece today.
I'd love to hear about integrations and onboarding.

My team handles that, and I believe Saad is, gonna be tasking my team with handling that for Maven, so I'd love to hear about that, but honestly, if I could get, like, a refresher on the product and what all you guys offer, that would be fantastic, too.
Yeah.
Yeah, we can definitely start with that.
So we… we've been… we have, we just added a big Phoenix group that we're onboarding right now.
So with that addition, it brings us to just, like, 100 properties exactly.
So I'll show you.
We have a couple different products that live under the Maven name, and we've been partnering with Asset on two of them.
So… Okay.
I'll just start with every product that we have under the Maven name is built to automate processes that teams are currently manually doing, and built to optimize profiles.
So, it's really built to streamline processes, fully fill out your profiles, get more property-specific, relevant content fed directly from your feeds to your property, or to your profiles.
And then we have an AI tool that we layer on top of that that helps to create the content.
Okay.
So, the products that we have in the suite are the Google Business AI tool, that's the one that we've been working with Asset the longest on.
Then we recently introduced the… our social automation tool.
So we've included that, or we're rolling that out with the… the Phoenix group that we've been working on.
are working with on, and that is a similar concept to Google, where it's automation first, directly from the feed to your profiles, but really built to save your team's time from posting.
Okay.
So those are the two, that we are working with your company on, and then we also have a reputation management tool and a listings management tool.
We've talked a little bit with the new dev team, about those two, but as of today, we're just partnering on these first two.
Okay, yeah, I'd love to know more about the Google Business, because I assumed that was kind of in tandem with the listings.
So, if I could kind of understand how those are a little bit different, that would be great.
Yep.
Yeah, I can definitely run through that.
Okay.
What do you do with listings, Jacqueline?
I'm sorry?
What product are you used to when it comes to listings?
Yeah, so we utilize Opinion heavily, you guys probably are aware of that, for reputation, and we have some properties on with our Social Pro, but they also have a Listings Pro product.
That we were gonna start pushing pretty heavily in 2026.
so… that's… that's more of what I'm familiar with.
And again, it's like, you know, one dashboard, it syndicates out to, like, all of the top spots, all the top… places that you would want to have your listings updated and accurate.
So, I'd love to see, kind of, what you guys offer in that space, too.
Just for a comparison to…

Well, we found with… there's a ton of listings management products out there, We have one, obviously, as part of our suite that… basically allows for your name, address, phone number to be correct and syndicated across Google, Bing, Apple, Siri, all of these other channels.
Yep.
really important for local SEO.
This Google Business AI product Significantly builds on that.
Okay.
we're gonna update… Kylie will walk you through it to you, but we're gonna send an… publish content daily onto your Google Business profile.
This is what kind of got Saad and his team interested in the first place.
A lot of teams manually post on Google, or manually update their pricing.
Everything you'll see here completely automates that every single time.
Okay.
onto Google.
And so… That's become a must-have for marketers in the industry.
You have to update your Google business profile, it's just really hard to do so manually.
And again, this is in addition to your listings.
Like, listings is basic, you gotta do it, this is the next step for your Google profile.
That's what… so, like, to your point, this would include the pricing, any type of, like, content, okay.
And do you guys… you guys obviously integrate with a PMS system for that?
Okay, got it.
So the integrations piece is as simple as turning on the feed syndication, and then allowing us Google management access.
So it's those two steps, and after that, we really take it over from there.
Okay, and I assume, like, you guys can get beads from, like, RealPage, Yardi, all of that, okay, all of the top ones, okay, perfect.
Yep.
Yeah, and then the areas that we're automating or optimizing throughout the profile are… we're doing daily automated posts.
So, every day, pulling the amenity-based information, photos, all directly from the feed, and then our tool creates a post every day.
Okay.
And then the products feed, I would say this is the piece that has gotten most of your team the most excited.
And this is a direct feed from your feed to the profile to show live pricing and availability.
Yep.
So, that's everyday updated pricing and availability directly from the feed.
what kind of oversight does the on-site team have for, like, the content that's generated?
Do they… are you guys seeing that they log in and they tweak it, or do they have to approve it?
What's the process with that?
We do have a whole dashboard experience where they can view all of the automated posts in one place.
They can see the next 30 days of automated posts, they can make changes in there if they want to, so they can edit it, deny a post.
They can also manually create posts in that dash.
But I will say we don't have many from Asset that are logging in, because… and the reason is because it's… the tool's built to replace the need for them to do that.
Right.
They always do have the ability to go in and make edits or changes if they want to, but the tool's built to replace that, so…
Yeah, hands-on, yeah.
Yeah, yep.
Okay.
Yeah.
the AI content's really good.
Like, AI's come a long way.
It's… it's really good, and it can be… adjusted for brand voice, too, if you have, like, a brand kit at each property.
It's really dialed in.
We found a lot of our customers They roll this out because it's significantly better than their manual posting, and it's more consistent as well.
Yeah, I believe that.
But for those that have an on-site team, or maybe somebody's taken ownership and does a great job posting, we do allow for them to post through our website.
Okay.
Okay, that's good to at least have the ability.
Yes.
The other couple areas that we're automating throughout the profile is concessions.
So this has also been a big one for your team, because we can pull concession data directly from the feed as well.
I've just pulled up a property here so I can show you.
I think it makes a little bit more sense to walk through it live.
But the… the concessions will come up in the post, so it's one concession… one post a week will be about a concession.
Here's an example of one.
So you can see the posts that are coming in are pulling in photos, both interior, like, exterior community-level amenities, but then in this area is where we're pulling in concession data as well.
So, there's a lot of…
3 weeks.
Time and effort and thought goes into creating these concessions, so this is one more place we can be advertising that concession.
And this is all AI-generated?
Yep.
AI-generated, but we're pulling the direct wording that you have in your feed here.
So we're taking what you have entered in your feed, that's like going out to ILSs, same terminology, just bringing it over to the profile.
Okay.
Perfect.
And then these concession posts will be removed as soon as that concession's removed from your feed.
So we're not storing any outdated…
Okay, that's good to know.
How often are you guys getting a feed?
For… we're pinging it at least once a day.
There's some… there's some feeds that allow us to pull a little bit more often.
Rent Cafe is one.
Bye.
It allows us to pull 4 times a day.
Okay, okay.
We'll ping it, so that pricing is updated 4 times.
I believe Entrada's still once a day.
But if needed, I mean, they could obviously log into the dashboard and pull that down if the client said, we want that concession off there ASAP.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, and our team can also manually override if there's anything that needs to be removed or changed, we can do that too.
Okay.
Sure.

Yeah, I think the main… the main benefit of this product and why it's continued to grow with an asset and other portfolios of similar size and scale is just the complete hands-off automation on a really important channel.
Yeah, I love that.
I mean, obviously, you guys, you guys probably have heard this, but unfortunately, we can't rely on on-site teams to get this kind of stuff done, so… and rightfully so.
I mean, they're busy on-site, but it's important.
So this is great that it is the hands-off, but you still have the ability to log in and tweak things if needed, but hopefully, to your point, it's hands-off.
Yep.
Yeah, I did want to show the products feed as well, so…
I know this is the piece that got the new dev team the most excited, was showing all available floor plans.
Right now.

The floor plan names displayed here, and then this is reflecting the lowest price per that floor plan type.
Okay.
Great, yeah, that's what we would want.
Yep.
Perfect.
This is another area we can be pushing those amenity keywords.
So, really, anywhere we can be pushing amenity keywords to help with your organic traffic, we'll be doing that as well.
Okay, I love that.
I was curious, Jacqueline… oh, sorry, Kylie.
But…
If you… you can finish out this part.
I had a question about onboarding.
This is just… I just wanted to point out that we're pulling in amenities as well.

But for properties that have all of their photo shoots done, this will be pulling in unit-level amenities and community-level amenities.
So usually you'll see, like, 6 to 10 different tiles here, highlighting amenities throughout the property.
I was gonna ask if there's, like, a max, how many you guys can pull in there.
Mmm…
This is the picture's for it.
Okay.
Yeah, I don't know if I've seen a max.
I've seen some that have, like, a list of at least 15 community-level amenities.
Okay.
Yeah.
Great.
I was gonna ask, so it sounds like you're very familiar with opinion.
What else is in your world, Jacqueline, that you're kind of… over the onboarding and implementation of that asset?
Really?

Really, it's just reputation, social, and listings management.
My team, helps, obviously, with the onboarding and implementation of those products, and then we work hand-in-hand to, typically with the vendor to do, like, lead trainings.
For the on-site folks.
Other than that, my team handles creative and rebrands, so any requests coming in for Print and promotional items and stuff like that.
We handle as well.
And does your… does your team work directly with the marketing team, or with also the operations side?
Who knows?
how does that work?
Like, for example, we worked with Lindsay Duffy in Phoenix, so… Is that somebody that you would work with as well, or does Lindsay go to marketing, who goes to you?
Like, how does that work?
So, it just depends if they have direct marketing.
If they do, then their marketing manager would then come to my team and request the onboarding.
But in a lot of cases, I want to say Lindsay has a portfolio that does not have a direct marketing person, so she would then come to us, and we would work directly with you guys to implement.
Who do we work with, Kylie, with Lindsay?
I'm strong, I'm totally…
Christy Powers is who's been.
Oh, okay.
I believe so.
Yep, she's the senior marketing manager, so she would come to us, and we would help implement.

And then you're saying you're using the term managed and unmanaged, so for some properties that don't have a marketing.
that would be the operations folks would say, hey, this is what I want to do, and you would be in charge of kind of helping spearhead that.
Correct.

Okay.

Okay.
That's great.

Fantastic.
Yep.
Okay.
Makes sense.
This is great.

cheat?

for companies like Asset, we had, you know, some that would roll out a big group of properties, but then they would trickle in, and it would be, like, you know, 10 properties, 15 properties, 1 property at a time.

Smartsheet, this is what you're used to?

in this meeting?
